content.Text: [Wed 2026-04-22 17:09 PDT] Create a new dashboard page `dashboard/triage.html` for the AgentLens observability platform. This is a **Smart Triage Queue** - an autonomous incident prioritization dashboard. ## What it does - Generates simulated incidents (errors, latency spikes, budget overruns, SLA breaches, anomalies, security events) for AI agents - Each incident gets an auto-computed **triage score** (0-100) based on weighted factors: severity (40%), blast radius (25%), recency (20%), recurrence (15%) - Incidents are displayed in a priority queue, sorted by triage score - Auto-triage mode: periodically re-scores and re-ranks, auto-escalates incidents that worsen, auto-resolves stale low-severity ones - Each incident shows: type icon, agent name, description, triage score badge (color-coded), time, affected agents count, recommended action - Filter by: type, severity, status (open/escalated/auto-resolved) - Triage score breakdown on click (shows each factor's contribution) - Stats bar: total open, escalated count, auto-resolved count, mean triage score, highest-risk agent - Canvas chart: triage score distribution histogram - Auto-triage toggle button with configurable interval - Proactive recommendations panel: "Top 3 actions to reduce queue" based on pattern analysis ## Style - Match the existing dashboard style: use the same `styles.css` link, same header pattern with nav links, same dark theme with cards - Look at dashboard/index.html for the header pattern - copy the full header with all nav links and add a "🚦 Triage" link - Use the same CSS classes: `.header`, `.main`, `.card`, `.btn`, `.status-dot`, etc. - Color scheme for triage scores: Critical (≥80) = red #ff4757, High (60-79) = orange #ffa502, Medium (40-59) = yellow #eccc68, Low (<40) = green #2ed573 ## Technical - Single self-contained HTML file with inline JS (no external deps except styles.css) - Generate 25-40 realistic simulated incidents on load - Canvas element for the histogram chart - All interactive - filters, sorting, auto-triage toggle, click-to-expand details ## Also do these: 1. Add a nav link `🚦 Triage` to the header in `dashboard/index.html` (add it after the Canary link, before the alerts button) 2. Add the same triage link to ALL other dashboard HTML files' headers (alerts.html, canary.html, command-center.html, compliance.html, correlator.html, cost-anomaly.html, costs.html, dependencies.html, diff.html, errors.html, postmortem.html, profiler.html, regression.html, replay.html, runbooks.html, scorecards.html, sla.html, timeline.html, topology.html, waterfall.html) ## Build verification After creating the file, run: `node -c dashboard/triage.html 2>&1 || echo "Note: HTML not JS, checking inline script extraction"` - just make sure the HTML is well-formed. Do NOT create any pull requests. Just commit with message: "feat(dashboard): Smart Triage Queue - autonomous incident prioritization with auto-scoring, escalation, and proactive recommendations" Then push directly: `git push origin HEAD:main`
I’m inspecting the existing dashboard files first so the new page and header updates match the current structure and styling before I edit anything.

I’ve confirmed the base header structure and shared stylesheet. Next I’m checking whether the other dashboard pages share the same header markup so I can patch them consistently and keep the new page aligned with existing components.

The header implementations are not uniform. `index.html` has the full global nav, while some pages use simplified page headers or back links, so I’m mapping each file’s structure before I patch them to avoid breaking any custom layout.

ToolUse.name: Run $files = 'alerts.html','canary.html','command-center.html','compliance.html','correlator.html','cost-anomaly.html','costs.html','dependencies.html','diff.html','errors.html','index.html','postmortem.html','profiler.html','regression.html','replay.html','runbooks.html','scorecards.html','sla.html','timeline.html','topology.html','waterfall.html'; foreach($f in $files){ Write-Output "--- $f ---"; Get-Content -Path (Join-Path 'dashboard' $f) -TotalCount 70 | Out-String | Write-Output }
